zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de Bryen於大約1325年出生在英國薩默塞特霍爾瓦時，她的父親Sir Guy Bryan38歲，母親Lady Joan Carew de Brienne31歲。她於1349年與John Cary在英國英格蘭薩默塞特霍爾瓦結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1356年在她的家鄉享年32歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane de Bryen于大约1325年出生在英国薩默塞特霍爾瓦时，她的父亲Sir Guy Bryan38岁，母亲Lady Joan Carew de Brienne31岁。她于1349年与John Cary在英国英格兰薩默塞特霍爾瓦结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1356年在她的家乡享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bryen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brennan, Conan, John Patrick, Kiera, Niall.
Irish: either a shortened form of McBreen or an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Braoin ‘descendant of Braon’, a byname meaning ‘moisture, drop’.
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Bre(d)en, from Old Norse breithr ‘wide’ + -en from vin ‘meadow’.

variants.name: Breene
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brennan.
Irish: variant of   Breen  .

variants.name: Brean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille.
English: habitational name from Brean in Somerset. The etymology of the placename is uncertain.
Irish: variant of   Breen   or   Brain  .

variants.name: Bruen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Finbarr.
Irish: variant of   Breen  .

North German (Brün): from Middle Low German brūn ‘brown’, hence probably a nickname for someone with brown hair or a dark complexion or for someone who habitually wore brown clothes. Compare Braun and Brun .

variants.name: McBreen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Braoin ‘son of Braon’, a personal name meaning ‘drop (of liquid)’. Compare Breen and Brennan .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de Bryen was born about 1325, in Holway, Somerset, England, her father, Sir Guy Bryan, was 38 and her mother, Lady Joan Carew de Brienne, was 31. She married John Cary in 1349, in Holway, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1356, in her hometown, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane de Bryen est née environ 1325, à Holway, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir Guy Bryan, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Lady Joan Carew de Brienne, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé John Cary en 1349, à Holway, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1356, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane de Bryen um 1325 in Holway, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Guy Bryan 38 und ihre Mutter Lady Joan Carew de Brienne 31. Sie hat 1349 in Holway, Somerset, England, United Kingdom John Cary geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1356 im Alter von 32 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Bryen è nata il circa 1325 nel Holway, Somerset, England, sua padre, Sir Guy Bryan, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Lady Joan Carew de Brienne, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato John Cary nel 1349, nel Holway, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1356, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 32 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane de Bryenが約1325年にイギリスソメルセトホールワイで生まれた時，父Sir Guy Bryanは38歳，母Lady Joan Carew de Brienneは31歳であった。1349年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトホールワイでJohn Caryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1356年に故郷で32歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de Bryen(이)가 대략 1325년 영국 서머세트 홀웨이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Guy Bryan(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joan Carew de Brienne(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1349년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 홀웨이에서 John Cary(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1356년 그녀의 고향에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de Bryen nasceu aproximadamente 1325, em Holway, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Guy Bryan, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joan Carew de Brienne, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com John Cary em 1349, em Holway, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1356, em sua cidade natal, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de Bryen родилась около 1325 г. в местности холвеи, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Guy Bryan было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joan Carew de Brienne, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cary в 1349 г. в местности холвеи, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1356 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane de Bryen nació aproximadamente 1325, en Holway, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Guy Bryan, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Lady Joan Carew de Brienne, tenía 31 años. Se casó con John Cary en 1349, en Holway, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1356, en su pueblo, a la edad de 32 años.
